LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT ADAM SHRYOCK'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS GRANTED LEAVE TO: 1) CLARIFY WHETHER SHE IS SEEKING ATTORNEY'S FEES UNDER THE LABOR CODE AND/OR PURSUANT TO A CONTRACT BETWEEN THE PARTIES; 2) TO REMOVE THE REQUEST FOR A SPECIFIC AMOUNT OF MONEY IN THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS; AND 3) TO CLARIFY THAT SHE IS SEEKING INTEREST PURSUANT TO CIVIL CODE 3289. THE LOSS OF USE ALLEGATIONS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT ADAM SHRYOCK MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. DENIED. ON A MOTION TO STRIKE, THE COURT MAY ONLY CONSIDER THE PLEADINGS AND MATTERS WHICH CAN BE JUDICIALLY NOTICED. THE PARTIES' CONTRACT IS NOT SUBJECT TO JUDICIAL NOTICE. THE ALLEGATIONS OF PARAGRAPHS 35 AND 75 OF THE COMPLAINT APPEAR TO HAVE SOME NEXUS TO MS. GAZICH'S CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. THE REQUEST TO STRIKE THESE PARAGRAPHS APPEARS TO BE A DISGUISED MOTION IN LIMINE OR MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND PLAINTIFF'S CONCERNS ARE BETTER ADDRESSED IN THE CONTEXT OF THOSE MOTIONS, NOT A MOTION TO STRIKE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-04-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS DEFENDANT ADAM SHRYOCK MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Denied. On a motion to strike, the Court may only consider the pleadings and matters which can be judicially noticed. The parties' contract is not subject to judicial notice. The allegations of paragraphs 35 and 75 of the complaint appear to have some nexus to Ms. Gazich's claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. The request to strike these paragraphs appears to be a disguised motion in limine or motion for protective order and plaintiff's concerns are better addressed in the context of those motions, not a motion to strike. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft'S Resonses To Set Two Of Pltf 'S Form Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft And Deft'S Attorney For $790
Off Calendar Mar-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL DEVOY OF JONES & DEVOY, LLP, TEL. NO. 615-0879 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. WILLIS C. SILVERTHORNE, TEL. NO. 781-0717 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS ADAM SHRYOCK, SEVEN GROUP LLC AND BOOBIES ROCK! INCORPORATED'S Amended Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Set Two Of Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft And Deft'S Attorney For $790- Defendant served answers to the second set of plaintiff's form interrogatories on February 8, 2012. Accordingly, the only matter before the Court is plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions. That request is granted. A monetary sanction of $790.00 is assessed against defendant and her counsel, jointly and severally, with payment to be made within 20 calendar days of the entry of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Protem Judge: Jim Weixel, Not reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel Devoy, Esq., Jones & Devoy LLP, 415-615-0879, for Cross-defendant and moving party

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted.
CROSS DEFENDANT ADAM SHRYOCK'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Granted with ten days leave to amend. Cross-complainant is granted leave to: 1) clarify whether she is seeking attorney's fees under the Labor Code and/or pursuant to a contract between the parties; 2) to remove the request for a specific amount of money in the punitive damages allegations; and 3) to clarify that she is seeking interest pursuant to Civil Code 3289. The loss of use allegations are adequately pled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-13-2012 Continued To Mar-29-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel Devoy, Esq., from Jones & Devoy, LLP, 415-615-0879, for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendants and moving party

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted. CROSS-DEFENDANT ADAM SHRYOCK'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.
Sustained, with ten days leave to amend, as to the third, fifth, sixth and seventh causes of action.
Overruled as to the first, second, fourth, eighth, ninth and tenth causes of action as Cross-Complainant has pled sufficient facts.
For the third cause of action, Cross-Complainant must allege whether the contract was oral, written or implied by conduct. For the fifth cause of action, Cross-Complainant has not alleged that Cross-Defendants' conduct was outrageous or a substantial factor in causing her severe emotional distress. For the sixth cause of action, Cross-Complainant has not alleged that the statements made were false. For the seventh cause of action, Cross-Complainant has not pled a legally protected privacy interest, a reasonable expectation of privacy in the circumstances, and conduct by Cross-Defendants constituted a serious invasion of privacy.
Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel Devoy, Esq., from Jones & Devoy, LLP, 415-615-0879, for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendants and moving party

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted. CROSS DEFENDANTS ADAM SHRYOCK'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is Granted, with ten days leave to amend, as to the prayer for attorney fees. Cross-Complainant seeks leave to amend to cite the specific statutory authority for attorney fees. Granted, with ten days leave to amend, as to allegations regarding punitive damages (pararaphs 79, 68, and 95, and paragraphs 5, 6, and 7 of the prayer for relief). Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918
